source_detail: Average Effective Tariff Rate — Policy through November 17, 2025 (Pre-Substitution)
title: Average Effective Tariff Rate
subtitle: Policy through November 17, 2025, Pre-Substitution
methodology: Each data point is the headline overall pre-substitution average effective US tariff rate reported by the Yale Budget Lab in a 'State of U.S. Tariffs' update as of the given date. Pre-substitution means the rate is computed by applying announced tariff schedules to the pre-tariff import mix (no behavioral shifting).

points.source: State of U.S. Tariffs: Week of April 7, 2025

points.note: China Tranche 1: 10% on all Chinese imports effective

points.source: State of U.S. Tariffs: Week of April 7, 2025
Fiscal and Economic Effects of the Revised April 9 Tariffs

points.note: EU reciprocal announcement / auto tariff additions
Court of International Trade ruling invalidated IEEPA tariffs May 28
Federal Circuit stay reinstates IEEPA tariffs pending appeal
Reciprocal letters and August 1 deadline tariffs queued
August 1 deadline tariffs effective; 16.2pp above baseline
Select product exemptions and revised Japan/EU deals

notes: Data points are the headline overall pre-substitution 'average effective US tariff rate' as reported by the Yale Budget Lab in each 'State of U.S. Tariffs' update through November 17, 2025.

'Pre-substitution' means the rate applies announced schedules to the pre-tariff import mix and does not adjust for consumer/supplier substitution away from higher-tariff goods. Yale's post-substitution rate on Nov 17, 2025 was 14.4%.

Notable events: April 2 'Liberation Day' universal baseline, April 9 revised reciprocal rates (peak ~28%), May 12 US–China 90-day pause, May 28 Court of International Trade ruling invalidating IEEPA tariffs (briefly dropped rate to ~6.9%), Federal Circuit stay restoring the tariffs on appeal, and the November 2025 agricultural exemptions.
Source: Yale Budget Lab — 'State of U.S. Tariffs' series and 'Tariff Rate Tracker' (Budget-Lab-Yale/Tariff-ETRs on GitHub).
